compiled_state.title: #2 Step 4: next_batch — in-process spike or defer (timeboxed)
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description: ticket-next-batch.sh is a ~900-line bash orchestrator (graph + file-impact) and the least latency-sensitive read. Timebox a spike to port it to a package fn; if cost/risk is high, LEAVE on subprocess and record the decision here. Do not let it block Steps 0/2/3 or straddle indefinitely. AC: explicit ported-or-deferred decision recorded.

comments.body: DECISION: DEFER — next_batch stays on the subprocess path. ticket-next-batch.sh is a ~900-line bash orchestrator (dependency graph + file-impact conflict scheduling) and the least latency-sensitive read (invoked once per batch dispatch, not in tight loops). Porting it in-process now carries high regression risk for negligible latency benefit. Re-evaluate only if/when a full Python engine port (#3) is undertaken; the native reads (#2) do not depend on it.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
